title: Pilosella abscissa
taxonomy: Pilosella abscissa is classified within the family Asteraceae, which includes daisies and sunflowers. The genus Pilosella is a group of plants that are commonly known as false dandelions. Pilosella abscissa is further classified as a member of the Pilosella section, which is a subset of plants within the genus. It is native to Europe and is also found in other parts of the world. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Pilosella abscissa shares characteristics with other plants in this family, such as having composite flowers.
uses: Pilosella abscissa, also known as a subspecies of the more commonly known Pilosella officinarum, has been utilized in traditional medicine due to its potential anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's extracts have been studied for their possible effects on various health conditions, including pain relief and inflammation. In some European folk medicine traditions, Pilosella abscissa has been used to treat conditions such as fever and rheumatism. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects. It is essential to note that the use of Pilosella abscissa for medicinal purposes should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a medical professional.

intro: Pilosella abscissa, also known as abscession's mouse-ear, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, Pilosella abscissa has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its potential to reduce pain and promote digestion. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its pharmacological properties. Pilosella abscissa is typically prepared as a tincture or infusion in traditional herbal medicine, and is often combined with other plants to enhance its therapeutic effects.
